title: centralized login/pwd, usable on a set of connections
author: vinsse2001
body_snippet:
Hello, thanks for this very useful tool :) !

So, here is a request for a new feature: 
=> **Instead of typing user/pwd in each connection**, it would be very useful to be able to **choose in a list of accounts**. So, when we need to update password, we just do it in accounts list, not in each connection.

## Expected Behavior
In connection configuration panel, I have the choice between typing login and password, or choosing an existing profile.
And somewhere else (in a new window, or in ...

iterations.description: Introduce a mechanism to link connections to centralized CredentialRecord objects stored in a CredentialRepository, allowing credential reuse and central management.

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: such functionality has been planed with connection profiles, what will give you ability to assign profile to machine without even knowing a password credentials, and depending on selected central storadge control them by you company policy removing such headache from admin completly :)

notes:
AI triage (gemini): Introduce a mechanism to link connections to centralized CredentialRecord objects stored in a CredentialRepository, allowing credential reuse and central management.
Approach: 1. Modify AbstractConnectionRecord to include a CredentialSource enum (Default, Specified, Linked) and make it inheritable. 2. In ConnectionInfo, adjust Username, Password, and Domain property getters to retrieve values from a CredentialRecord via CredentialServiceFacade when CredentialSource is Linked and a valid CredentialId is present. 3. Ensure CredentialId and CredentialSource are serialized/deserialized with connections. 4. Handle SecureString conversion for passwords during retrieval from CredentialRecord.
